summary: The docker/bake-action build failed because a wget command downloading the nydus static binary from GitHub returned an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway error. This caused the Dockerfile build step to exit with code 1, aborting the entire integration-tests image build.
root_cause: A transient HTTP 502 Bad Gateway error from GitHub prevented the nydus release artifact download during a Docker build RUN step at Dockerfile line 336.

evidence.quote: wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: The wget command received a 502 from GitHub, indicating a transient network/server error that caused the download to fail.

evidence.quote: ERROR: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: This is the direct build error propagated from the failed wget, confirming the download failure terminated the build step.
Identifies the exact failing Dockerfile line (336) where the nydus release is fetched.

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: GitHub Actions surfaced this as the terminal error, confirming the download failure is the root cause of the job failure.

suggested_fix: Re-run the job, as this is a transient 502 error from GitHub's release download endpoint. If it recurs, add retry logic around the wget command in the Dockerfile (e.g., using a retry loop or switching to a curl-based download with --retry flags).

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
